content:
Hello! I'm bit new to this, but I've been exploring to model PCI config-space abstractions, as mentioned in docs/gpu/nova/todo.

PCI MISC APIs
Extend the existing PCI device / driver abstractions by SR-IOV, config space,
capability, MSI API abstractions.

Complexity: Beginner

So far, I've:

  • read the PCI spec (focused on config space and capabilities)
  • tinkered with relevant C APIs
  • read some existing rust abstractions

I had a couple of questions:

  1. Just to be sure, is the goal here mainly to expose the pci_(read/write)_config_(byte/word/dword) methods only? Or are there other methods expected, specifically for use within Nova?

  2. For typed access to PCI config space, will register! macro be eventually used? Or just expose raw values and have the caller deal with typed access? If it's the latter, does this API seem reasonable?

let config = ConfigSpace::new::<REGION>(pdev); // REGION: Standard/Extended
let value: Result<T> = config.read::<T>(offset); // T: u8/u16/u32 (or byte/word/dword newtypes)
let output: Result<()> = config.write::<T>(offset, value);

Any guidance would be appreciated :)
Ritvik Gupta has marked this topic as resolved.
